content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh

## Summary

Accept the amendment. Relying on PID existence alone for legacy snapshots is an unsafe liveness signal and can indefinitely misclassify stale records as running while blocking recovery paths.

## Votes

- `fr-25-6d0379b7`: Accept
  The proposed change correctly addresses a real correctness and reliability issue: non-authoritative legacy pid-only records lack identity metadata (proc_start_ticks), so kill(pid,0) only proves PID reuse by any process, not ownership by the tracked run. This can leave status reporting inconsistent with resume/stop behavior and create unrecoverable false-Running states. Requiring a second ownership proof (or degrading such records to stale) is the safer, more maintainable model and aligns status with recovery semantics.
